Action.input_port: Whether to remove the object with the given ID. This supercedes the replace parameter
Whether to replace an existing object with the same id or to append to it
The identifier for the object. Useful for referring to it later on
The frame_id of the mesh. shape_msgs/Mesh doesn't store a header by default
An absolute or URI filepath to a mesh object in a supported format
The mesh of the object to be placed into the scene
The namespace to prepend to the moveit service name
Time in seconds to wait for the servers to appear
A whitespace-separated list of fully qualified server names to check for
Time to wait for a response in seconds
Time to wait for the service to be discovered in seconds
If set to true, clear outside the radius instead of inside
The radius around the robot to clear
The type of costmap to clear. Options are [global, local]
The service to call

Action.output_port: The output pointcloud if 'override' is set to false
Action.input_port: The second pointcloud to concatenate
The first pointcloud to concatenate
Cancel goal after N seconds (default 10 min)
Absolute filepath of a .xml file describing a BT.CPP tree.
Raw text in XML format describing a BT.CPP tree.
The string to prepend to '/follow_joint_trajectory' in the action server name
The trajectory to execute

Decorator.output_port: The number of items that were in the container
The index of the current item being processed
Decorator.inout_port: Flag to break out of the loop
Decorator.input_port: The delimiter to use for separating string inputs. Default to semicolon

Decorator.output_port: The number of items that were in the container
The index of the current item being processed
Decorator.inout_port: Flag to break out of the loop
Decorator.input_port: The delimiter to use for separating string inputs. Default to semicolon

Decorator.output_port: The number of items that were in the container
The index of the current item being processed
Decorator.inout_port: Flag to break out of the loop
Decorator.input_port: The delimiter to use for separating string inputs. Default to semicolon

Decorator.output_port: The number of items that were in the container
The index of the current item being processed
Decorator.inout_port: Flag to break out of the loop
Decorator.input_port: The delimiter to use for separating string inputs. Default to semicolon

Decorator.output_port: The number of items that were in the container
The index of the current item being processed
Decorator.inout_port: Flag to break out of the loop
Decorator.input_port: The delimiter to use for separating string inputs. Default to semicolon

Decorator.output_port: The number of items that were in the container
The index of the current item being processed
Decorator.inout_port: Flag to break out of the loop
Decorator.input_port: The delimiter to use for separating string inputs. Default to semicolon

Action.input_port: Whether or not the reference frame is static or dynamic. Dynamic poses have a finite lifetime. Default True
Float time in seconds to wait for the transform to appear. Default 2 seconds
The frame whose location to record
The frame in which to locate the robot. Defaults to 'map'

Action.output_port: The measured pose of the requested link if the robot is at base_pose
Action.input_port: Float time in seconds to wait for the transform to appear. Default 2 seconds
The pose of some other link on the robot
The name of the base frame to use for the transform lookup
The frame whose location to record

Action.output_port: The message value (in shared ptr form) received from the topic
Action.input_port: The type of the message. If omitted it will be inferred from existing topics
Whether or not the topic to subscribe to is published transient_local. Default false
The maximum time in second to wait for a message. Default 5.0 seconds
Whether or not to subscribe with a best-effort reliability policy. Default false
Fully qualified ROS topic to subscribe to
The time to wait for the scene to be supplied. Note that large collision scenes can take some time.
The namespace under which to put the collision scene service call. Default no namespace

Action.output_port: The solution joint state for the robot
Action.input_port: The name of the link for which we want to solve IK. Default to the group end effector
Whether or not the IK request should consider the collision objects in the scene
The desired pose of the IK link
The name of the group for which to request an IK solution
Action.output_port: The solution joint state for any multi-dof joints in the robot
Action.input_port: How long to wait for the request to return in seconds
The namespace of the move_group node

Action.output_port: The blackboard value to set with the value of the ROS parameter
Action.input_port: Whether to set the output value as a shared pointer. Default false
The type of the parameter. Options are 'auto', 'bool', 'string', 'double', 'int'. To specify an array, append '_array' like bool_array
The name of the parameter
The name of the node whose parameter we are getting
Value to make shared pointer from
Whether a partial plan should be executed before returning
The time to wait for the robot to complete its motion in seconds
The time to wait for the planner to compute in seconds
Maximum cartesian speed in m/s
The maximum distance between interpolated waypoints
The moveit planning group for which to plan
The link that we are planning for
The fully qualified action server name to call on. Overrides 'moveit_namespace' if set
Action.output_port: Whether or not the computer trajectory covers less that 100 percent of the waypoints
Action.input_port: The namespace to prepend to standard moveit service/action names
The maximum joint movement between interpolated waypoints
The set of poses to move the end effector through
The pose to move the end effector to
The axis about which to give rotational freedom. Leave blank for 6DoF pose. Options are [x, y, z]. Applies to sample-based planners only
Ratio of maximum allowed joint accelerations to those specified in the robot config
Maximum cartesian speed in m/s
The time to wait for the robot to complete its motion in seconds. Default unlimited
Ratio of maximum allowed joint velocities to those specified in the robot config
The time to wait for the planner to compute in seconds. Default 5 seconds
The max corner for the robot's allowable operating region. Omit for no limits
The pose to move the end effector to
The namespace to prepend to standard moveit service/action names
The fully qualified action server name to call on. Overrides 'moveit_namespace' for the action server if set. Moveit namespace may still be needed for the cartesian planner
The moveit planning group for which to plan
The link that we are planning for
The planning pipeline to use for planning
The planner to use
semicolon delimited constraints to place on the resulting end effector path. Options are [LINEAR, FIXED_ORIENTATION]
Number of plans to create before picking the shortest one. Default 1
Maximum number of dynamic replanning events if the path becomes infeasible during execution. Default 0
The min corner for the robot's allowable operating region. Omit for no limits
Time to wait before replanning events
Acceptable deviation from a fixed orientation path in degrees
Acceptable deviation from a linear path in meters. Values below 0.001 will be considered equality constraints
The speed factor by which to scale the resulting trajectory
The namespace to prepend to the action server topic name
Maximum planning time in seconds. Default 5 seconds
The MoveIt planning group to invoke
The joint state for the group to plan and move to
A value to evaluate as the resume condition
The code to evaluate as the resume condition
Whether the publisher should be transient local
The value to publish
Whether the publisher should be best effort
The topic to publish to
A pose representing the pose of the child frame in the parent frame. If this is set, only additionally set child frame
The y component of the transform rotation
The roll component of the transform rotation
The frame in which the transform is defined
The new frame to publish
The x coordinate of the transform translation
Whether the transform is a static transform
The w component of the transform rotation
The z component of the transform rotation
The y coordinate of the transform translation
The x component of the transform rotation
The z coordinate of the transform translation
The pitch component of the transform rotation
The yaw component of the transform rotation

Control.output_port: The current index of the repeated loop
Control.input_port: Whether the condition node should be run every tick, or just when restarting the repeated node
Optional maximum number of times to repeat before returning success

Action.input_port: The type of the parameter. Options are 'bool', 'string', 'double', 'int'. To specify an array, append '_array' like bool_array
The value to set the parameter
The name of the parameter
The name of the node whose parameter we are setting
Optional node to spin. If not set, it will spin the node used in this behavior's constructor

Decorator.output_port: The time taken for the child node to run in seconds

Action.input_port: Whether to use an Empty service type instead of a Trigger service type
Time in seconds to wait for a response
Fully qualified name of the service to call
The name of the service to use for service proceeding
Time to wait for user input in seconds. Default 0 (no timeout)
Whether or not to open stdin for proceeding
Whether or not to create a Trigger service for proceeding
